merged_events.event_description: The United States established its first consulate in what is now Samoa at Apia (the capital) when Jonathan S. Jenkins took up the post of Consul there.

evidence.quote: The United States established its first consulate in what is now Samoa at Apia when Jonathan S. Jenkins took up the post of Consul there on May 17, 1856.

merged_events.event_description: The U.S. consulate at Apia closed on September 23, 1927, ending consular representation in what is now Samoa.

The United States established diplomatic relations with Samoa when Ambassador Kenneth Franzheim II presented credentials at Apia, but the Ambassador remained resident at Wellington, New Zealand, making this a nonresident ambassadorial relationship.

evidence.quote: The United States and Samoa established diplomatic relations when Ambassador Kenneth Franzheim II presented his credentials to the Government of Samoa at Apia on July 14, 1971. Since 1971, the U.S. Ambassador to Samoa has also been accredited to New Zealand, and at various times also Fiji, and Tonga, and has remained resident at Wellington, New Zealand.

merged_events.event_description: The United States established a physical Embassy in Apia on November 15, 1988, though the Ambassador continued to reside at Wellington and the Embassy was led by a Chargé d'Affaires.

evidence.quote: The United States established an Embassy in Apia on November 15, 1988. However, the Ambassador has always remained resident at Wellington, and the Embassy has been under the leadership of a Chargé d'Affaires.

matched.notes: Perfect match. Both sources agree on May 17, 1856 as the date the U.S. consulate was established at Apia.
Perfect match. Both sources agree on September 23, 1927 as the date the consulate at Apia closed.
Perfect match. Both sources agree on July 14, 1971 as the date diplomatic relations were established with a nonresident ambassador accredited from Wellington.

Perfect match. Both sources agree on November 15, 1988 as the date the Embassy was established in Apia. Note that the ambassador remained resident at Wellington with the Embassy led by a Chargé d'Affaires.
